item.title: 【Building a Production-Ready AI Agent in 2026: Beyond the Hello World Demo】2026 年构建可投入生产的 AI 代理：超越 Hello World 演示
item.description:
【

The Demo Problem: The "Vibe" vs. The "System"

In 2026, the novelty of an AI agent answering a question has evaporated. Every developer can string together a "Hello World" demo using the latest Anthropic or OpenAI SDK. These demos usually look flawless on LinkedIn: the agent reads a PDF, summarizes it, and perhaps even "books a flight" in a mock environment.

However, the "Demo-to-Production Gap" is wider than ever. When these agents hit real users, they encounter edge cases that a notebook can't simulate:

】

演示问题：“氛围”与“系统”

到 2026 年，AI 代理回答问题的新颖性已经消失。每个开发人员都可以使用最新的 Anthropic 或 OpenAI SDK 拼凑出一个“Hello World”演示。这些演示在 LinkedIn 上通常看起来完美无缺：代理读取 PDF，对其进行总结，甚至可能在模拟环境中“预订航班”。

但是，“演示到生产的差距”比以往任何时候都更大。当这些代理接触到真实用户时，他们会遇到笔记本无法模拟的边缘情况：

item.title: 【Inside What Actually Breaks in Large-Scale S/4HANA Conversions (And How to Prevent It)】深入了解大规模 S/4HANA 转换中实际出现的问题（以及如何预防）
item.description:
【

Broken Custom ABAP Code in S/4HANA

From an engineer’s perspective, one of the first headaches in a brownfield S/4HANA migration is custom ABAP code that no longer runs correctly. Unlike a simple upgrade, S/4HANA introduces a new architecture with a simplified data model and revised logic. Many classic SAP ECC tables and transactions either vanish or behave differently in S/4HANA, meaning some Z-programs that worked fine in ECC may now short-dump or produce incorrect results.

Common breakage patterns include:

】

S/4HANA 中损坏的自定义 ABAP 代码

从工程师的角度来看，棕地 S/4HANA 迁移中最令人头痛的问题之一是自定义 ABAP 代码不再正确运行。与简单的升级不同，S/4HANA引入了新的架构，简化了数据模型，修改了逻辑。许多经典的 SAP ECC 表和事务在 S/4HANA 中要么消失，要么表现不同，这意味着一些在 ECC 中运行良好的 Z 程序现在可能会出现短路转储或产生不正确的结果。

常见破损模式包括：

item.title: 【When Angular APIs Return 200 but the Frontend Is Already Failing Users】当 Angular API 返回 200 但前端已经让用户失败时
item.description:
【

Successful HTTP requests have become a deceptively comforting metric in modern web systems. Dashboards show low latency, the network tab fills with green entries and the backend reports clean 2xx rates, yet users experience empty screens, contradictory state, stuck workflows or data that appears to randomly revert. This failure mode is common in Angular applications because the transport layer can succeed while the application layer has already violated a business contract and Angular’s default HTTP and reactive ergonomics are optimized around HTTP-level success versus domain-level correctness. 

How Angular Treats 200 as Success

Angular’s HTTP layer is intentionally aligned with HTTP semantics a request is represented as an Observable and failures in the HTTP layer are emitted on the Observable error channel. Angular documents three broad categories of request failure network/connection failure, timeout and backend error responses and states that HttpClient captures these errors as an HttpErrorResponse returned through the Observable’s error channel. When an API responds with a non success HTTP status, the error channel is used and HttpErrorResponse provides the HTTP layer context. 

】

成功的 HTTP 请求已成为现代 Web 系统中一个看似令人欣慰的指标。仪表板显示低延迟，网络选项卡充满绿色条目，后端报告干净的 2xx 速率，但用户会遇到空白屏幕、矛盾状态、工作流程停滞或似乎随机恢复的数据。这种失败模式在 Angular 应用程序中很常见，因为传输层可以成功，而应用程序层已经违反了业务合同，并且 Angular 的默认 HTTP 和反应式人体工程学是围绕 HTTP 级成功与域级正确性进行优化的。 

Angular 如何将 200 视为成功

Angular 的 HTTP 层有意与 HTTP 语义保持一致，请求表示为 Observable，HTTP 层中的失败会在 Observable 错误通道上发出。 Angular 记录了三大类请求失败、网络/连接失败、超时和后端错误响应，并指出 HttpClient 将这些错误捕获为通过 Observable 错误通道返回的 HttpErrorResponse。当 API 以非成功 HTTP 状态响应时，将使用错误通道，并且 HttpErrorResponse 提供 HTTP 层上下文。

item.title: 【Beyond SOLID: Embracing CUPID for Modern Software Craftsmanship】超越 SOLID：采用 CUPID 实现现代软件工艺
item.description:
【

For decades, the SOLID principles — Single Responsibility, Open-Closed, Liskov Substitution, Interface Segregation, and Dependency Inversion — have been the undisputed gold standard of object-oriented design. They were forged in an era of monolithic desktop applications and strict C++ or Java hierarchies.

However, as our industry has shifted toward microservices, serverless functions, and dynamic languages, many developers find that strictly following SOLID can lead to "over-engineering." We end up with an explosion of interfaces for single-method classes and a cognitive load that makes the codebase feel like a dense, impenetrable thicket.

】

几十年来，SOLID原则——单一职责、开闭、里氏替换、接口隔离和依赖倒转——一直是面向对象设计无可争议的黄金标准。它们是在单一桌面应用程序和严格的 C++ 或 Java 层次结构的时代铸造的。

但是，随着我们的行业转向微服务、无服务器函数和动态语言，许多开发人员发现严格遵循 SOLID 可能会导致“过度设计”。我们最终会遇到单方法类接口的爆炸式增长和认知负担，这使得代码库感觉像一个密集的、难以穿透的灌木丛。

item.title: 【The Only AI Test That Still Humbles Every Machine on Earth】唯一一个仍然让地球上每台机器都感到羞愧的人工智能测试
item.description:
【

Imagine a video game with no instructions. No tutorial. No hint of what winning even looks like. You get dropped in, and you figure it out.

Most people do this in under a minute.

】

想象一个没有说明的视频游戏。没有教程。甚至没有任何关于获胜的迹象。你被投入其中，然后你就明白了。

大多数人在一分钟内就能完成此操作。

item.title: 【Custom Model Context Protocol (MCP) for NL2SQL: A Rigorous Evaluation Framework on Oracle Database】NL2SQL 的自定义模型上下文协议 (MCP)：Oracle 数据库的严格评估框架
item.description:
【

When you let an LLM turn natural language into SQL, you need to know: is it correct, will it run on your database, and is it efficient? SQLclMCP is an open-source framework that answers those questions by comparing LLM-generated SQL to human-written baselines on Oracle Database — using the Model Context Protocol (MCP) and a 500-question TPC-H benchmark. MCP keeps “how SQL is generated” behind a single HTTP API: the evaluator sends a question and gets back SQL, so you can swap models, prompts, or even the server implementation and still run the same evaluation. This article walks through the pipeline, how to run it, what gets measured, a few example graphs and tables, and Oracle gotchas we fixed in the prompt.

Why This Matters

Natural language to SQL (NL2SQL) works well for ad-hoc questions and app backends — until the model returns the wrong rows or a query that fails or runs too slowly in production. To ship with confidence you need three guarantees: the result set is correct (same logical result as the intended query), the SQL executes on your database without syntax or runtime errors, and it’s efficient enough (reasonable latency and plan quality, e.g. Oracle EXPLAIN PLAN). The only reliable way to get those guarantees is to compare LLM output to a gold standard on a real database, in a repeatable pipeline — so you can improve prompts, compare models, and catch dialect gotchas (Oracle vs MySQL, EXTRACT vs LIMIT, and the like). This framework gives you that pipeline.

】

当你让LLM将自然语言转换为SQL时，你需要知道：它正确吗？ 在您的数据库上运行，它高效吗？ SQLclMCP 是一个开源框架，它通过使用模型上下文协议 (MCP) 和 500 个问题的 TPC-H 基准将 LLM 生成的 SQL 与 Oracle 数据库上人工编写的基线进行比较来回答这些问题。 MCP 将“SQL 的生成方式”保留在单个 HTTP API 后面：评估器发送问题并返回 SQL，因此您可以交换模型、提示甚至服务器实现，并且仍然运行相同的评估。本文将介绍该管道、如何运行它、测量什么、一些示例图形和表格，以及我们在提示中修复的 Oracle 问题。

为什么这很重要

自然语言转 SQL (NL2SQL) 非常适合临时问题和应用程序后端 — 直到模型返回错误的行或查询失败或在生产中运行太慢。为了充满信心地交付，您需要三个保证：结果集正确（与预期查询的逻辑结果相同）、SQL 在数据库上执行，没有语法或运行时错误，并且它足够高效（合理的延迟和计划质量，例如 Oracle EXPLAIN PLAN）。获得这些保证的唯一可靠方法是将 LLM 输出与可重复管道中的真实数据库上的黄金标准进行比较 - 这样您就可以改进提示、比较模型并捕获方言陷阱（Oracle 与 MySQL、EXTRACT 与 LIMIT 等）。这个框架为您提供了管道。

item.title: 【RAG Done Right: When to Use SQL, Search, and Vector Retrieval and How To Combine Them】RAG 做得对：何时使用 SQL、搜索和向量检索以及如何组合它们
item.description:
【

In this article, I will attempt to explain why retrieval-agumented generation (RAG) fails when retrieval is treated as a one-size-fits-all approach.

For example, the internal AI assistant looks great at demo time. Vector database ingesting overnight, GPT-4-class model, clean stakeholder presentation. The team ships.

】

在本文中，我将尝试解释为什么当检索被视为一种万能的方法时，检索论证生成 (RAG) 会失败。

例如，内部人工智能助手在演示时看起来很棒。矢量数据库隔夜摄取、GPT-4 级模型、干净的利益相关者演示。团队发货。

item.title: 【How AI Is Rewriting Full-Stack Java Systems: Practical Patterns with Spring Boot, Kafka and WebSockets】AI 如何重写全栈 Java 系统：Spring Boot、Kafka 和 WebSocket 的实用模式
item.description:
【

Building real-time applications means balancing user responsiveness with heavy backend processing. A proven solution is to decouple heavy workloads using events and asynchronous processing. In this approach, a Spring Boot application quickly publishes events to Kafka instead of processing requests inline. Then Kafka consumers (with AI/ML logic) handle the data in the background, and the results are pushed to clients in real time via WebSockets. This article highlights three key patterns enabling this architecture:

  1. Event Production with Spring Boot and Kafka
  2. AI-Driven Processing in Kafka Consumers
  3. Real-Time WebSocket Delivery to the Frontend

Event Production with Spring Boot and Kafka

The first step is capturing an event and publishing it to Kafka. By offloading work to Kafka the application can respond immediately to the user without waiting for processing. Spring Boot’s integration with Apache Kafka provides a KafkaTemplate to send messages to topics.

】

构建实时应用程序意味着在用户响应能力与繁重的后端处理之间取得平衡。行之有效的解决方案是使用事件和异步处理来解耦繁重的工作负载。在这种方法中，Spring Boot 应用程序快速将事件发布到 Kafka，而不是内联处理请求。然后K​​afka消费者（具有AI/ML逻辑）在后台处理数据，结果通过WebSockets实时推送到客户端。本文重点介绍了实现此架构的三个关键模式：

  1. 使用 Spring Boot 和 Kafka 进行事件生产
  2. Kafka 消费者中的人工智能驱动处理
  3. 实时 WebSocket 传输到前端

使用 Spring Boot 和 Kafka 进行事件生产

第一步是捕获事件并将其发布到 Kafka。通过将工作卸载到 Kafka，应用程序可以立即响应用户，而无需等待处理。 Spring Boot 与 Apache Kafka 的集成提供了一个 KafkaTemplate 来将消息发送到主题。

item.title: 【The Data Warehouse Concurrency Playbook: Surviving the "Super Bowl" Moment】数据仓库并发手册：度过“超级碗”时刻
item.description:
【

It was a normal Tuesday until someone dropped a real-time dashboard link into a big team group. A few people opened it, and then a few hundred did. Within minutes, a slack pattern appeared: queries timing out, dashboards spinning, and the inevitable 'Is the data broken?'.

The confusing part here is that the CPU wasn't paged, the warehouse didn't look obviously maxed out, and nothing was 'red.' Yet the platform was unusable. That's what concurrency incidents look like in data: not a clean failure but a slow collapse into queues and retries.

】

这是一个正常的星期二，直到有人将实时仪表板链接放入一个大团队组中。几个人打开了它，然后几百人打开了它。几分钟之内，出现了一种松弛模式：查询超时、仪表板旋转以及不可避免的“数据损坏了吗？”。

这里令人困惑的部分是 CPU 没有被分页，仓库看起来并没有明显达到最大，并且没有任何东西是“红色的”。但该平台无法使用。这就是并发事件在数据中的样子：不是彻底的失败，而是缓慢崩溃为队列和重试。

item.title: 【How to Make Software Team Deliver More, Faster and Better #1 - The Team Toolset】如何让软件团队交付更多、更快、更好#1 - 团队工具集
item.description:
【

Every engineering manager, VP or CTO demands their teams to push for more - quicker delivery, more features and with better quality. On the other hand side the guy with the project manager hat can simply laugh - up to those the delivery is a simple math and a balance between the scope (features, the More), the time  (the Faster) and the Better (or the Worse, say - the quality). Based on my experience as an engineer and an engineering manager I still think that there is a big box of tools that can be used to break the project management triangle and simply push things further - for more, faster and better. In the given article I will start with toolset#1 - the team.

Managers do not deliver themself, they deliver with their teams. So, the goal of the engineering manager is to make sure that their team deliver. And the toolset that you have is as follows:

】

每位工程经理、副总裁或首席技术官都要求他们的团队追求更多 - 更快的交付、更多的功能和更好的质量。另一方面，戴着项目经理帽子的人可以简单地笑 - 对于那些人来说，交付只是一个简单的数学和范围（功能，更多），时间（更快）和更好（或更差，比如说 - 质量）之间的平衡。根据我作为工程师和工程经理的经验，我仍然认为有一大箱工具可以用来打破项目管理三角关系，并简单地推动事情进一步发展 - 获得更多、更快、更好。在给定的文章中，我将从工具集#1 - 团队开始。

经理不是自己交付成果，而是与团队一起交付成果。因此，工程经理的目标是确保他们的团队交付成果。您拥有的工具集如下：
